description: NFL Draft history 2022-2026 with dynasty analysis

dynasty_notes.Fernando Mendoza: 1.01 LV. Indiana QB, mobile with strong arm. SF franchise QB candidate if he develops. Raiders need him badly.
dynasty_notes.Jeremiyah Love: 1.03 ARI. Notre Dame RB. Best dynasty RB in class. Explosive, receives well. ARI pass-heavy limits floor but ceiling elite.
dynasty_notes.Carnell Tate: 1.04 TEN. Ohio State WR. Surprise pick at 4. Elite route runner. Titans rebuilding — long runway, top dynasty prospect.
dynasty_notes.Jordyn Tyson: 1.08 NO. Arizona State WR. Big-play deep threat. Saints desperately need receivers. Immediate role potential.
dynasty_notes.Makai Lemon: 1.20 PHI. USC WR. Eagles traded up. Great org but AJ Brown/DeVonta Smith limit targets. Upside in year 2-3.
dynasty_notes.Ty Simpson: 1.13 LAR. Alabama QB. Stafford heir apparent. Dynasty timeline depends on when Stafford retires. SF leagues monitor.
dynasty_notes.Kenyon Sadiq: 1.16 NYJ. Oregon TE. Jets desperately need TE weapons. If he earns role early, high upside.
dynasty_notes.Jadarian Price: 1.32 SEA. Notre Dame RB. End of round 1 value. Seahawks need a bell cow. Late riser.
dynasty_notes.De'Zhaun Stribling: 2.33 SF. Mississippi WR. Shanahan system WR — historically produces dynasty value. Monitor heavily.
1.30 NYJ. Indiana WR. Jets needed a slot. Young Mendoza (if he starts) will need targets.
dynasty_notes.Nicholas Singleton: 5.165 TEN. Penn State RB. Titans adding RB depth after Carnell Tate pick. Late round flier.
dynasty_notes.Kaelon Black: 3.90 SF. Indiana RB. Another Shanahan system RB. SF system makes every RB here worth watching.
dynasty_notes.Carson Beck: 3.65 ARI. Miami QB. Cardinals backup QB behind Love/Kyler situation. Watch for opportunity.
dynasty_notes.KC Concepcion: 1.24 CLE. Texas A&M WR. Browns needed receivers. Deep sleeper.
dynasty_notes.Germie Bernard: 2.47 PIT. Alabama WR. Steelers traded up for him. Pittsburgh needs a real WR1 next to Pickens.

WR.elite_profile: 80+ catches OR 1000+ yards in final college season. 15+ yards per catch indicates downfield ability. 20%+ target share on team.
WR.red_flags: Low target share despite good stats (benefited from scheme). Didn't play against good defenses. Pure slot with no downfield threat.
WR.breakout_timing: Year 2-3 in NFL is typical WR breakout window. Year 1 = scheme learning. Year 3+ without breakout = ceiling hit.
WR.key_stats: College yards per route run, contested catch %, target share, dominator rating

RB.elite_profile: 800+ rushing yards AND 40+ receptions. 4.5+ yards per carry. Shown pass protection ability.
RB.red_flags: High carry load in college (injury risk). Pure between-the-tackles with no receiving role. Single year of production.
RB.breakout_timing: RBs can break out year 1 with right situation. Peak window age 23-26. Decline starts 27+.
RB.key_stats: Yards after contact, broken tackle rate, reception rate, college dominator rating

QB.elite_profile: 65%+ completion rate. 8.0+ yards per attempt. Mobility adds 15-20% value in SF. Strong arm + accuracy combo.
QB.red_flags: System QB (Air Raid only). Poor under pressure stats. Needed clean pocket. Low rushing ability in SF.
QB.breakout_timing: QBs typically need 2-3 years. Some break out year 2 (Daniels). Mobility fades with age.
QB.key_stats: CPOE (completion % over expected), pressure stats, yards per attempt, rushing contribution

TE.elite_profile: Receiving TE with 600+ yards in college. Good routes, good hands. 6'4"+ OR elite athleticism (RAS 8.0+).
TE.red_flags: Pure blocking TE. Low college production. Slow 40 time (4.8+) without compensating traits.
TE.breakout_timing: TEs are latest position to develop — typically year 3-4. Bowers/Kelce exceptions.
TE.key_stats: College target share, yards per route run, inline vs slot usage, blocking grade
